CBA_01.reason_no_frozen_concept_fit: The letter concerns discretionary telework eligibility and program governance; it is not schedule notice, no-cancellation security, regular hours, or a concrete remote-work benefit concept in the frozen library.
CBA_01.evidence_summary: Virtual Office is a cooperative manager-employee agreement, not an entitlement, and may be terminated by employee, manager, or Company at any time.

CBA_01.candidate_object: Travel card fee, delinquency, and credit-reporting protections
CBA_01.reason_no_frozen_concept_fit: The provision creates employee financial/credit protections for business travel cards; it is not a wage, reimbursement amount, privacy-records concept, or grievance concept as currently defined.
CBA_01.evidence_summary: Employees are not required to pay card company before reimbursement when delay is not their fault; employee neglect can trigger 2.5% plus $10 fee; credit-bureau reporting limited.

CBA_01.candidate_object: Personal retention of frequent flier mileage and travel-related benefits
CBA_01.reason_no_frozen_concept_fit: The provision is a travel-related perquisite/reimbursement-adjacent right not covered by current wage, allowance, or benefit concepts.
CBA_01.evidence_summary: Frequent flier mileage and travel-related benefits incurred on Company business are credited to personal employee accounts and may be applied to personal travel.

CBA_03.candidate_label: Conscientious objection to therapeutic abortion participation
CBA_03.reason_for_novelty: The frozen concept library lacks a precise concept for moral/religious objection to direct participation in specified medical procedures with protection from coercion, unreasonable transfer/assignment, or discipline. It is related to safety/working conditions and discipline but does not fit existing safety/PPE, assault, leave, or discipline concepts cleanly.
CBA_03.suggested_review_area: worker conscience/religious objection rights in health-care CBAs

CBA_04.why_no_frozen_concept_fits: The frozen library has child/dependent-care and parental/family leave concepts, but no adoption-expense reimbursement concept. The MOA provides up to $10,000 per adopted child but is not child care, dependent-care reimbursement, or leave.
CBA_04.evidence: Effective July 1, 2003, regular full or part-time CBA-covered employees may claim reimbursement of adoption expenses up to $10,000 per adopted child; plan administration and disputes rest with Company and are not grievable/arbitrable.
CBA_04.scoreability_note: CBA-contained reimbursement cap is present, but full plan terms and administration are external/company-discretionary.

CBA_04.source_pointer: pages 151-155 Public Communications Incentive Compensation Plan; pages 156-157 Retail Sales Consultant Compensation Plan
CBA_04.why_no_frozen_concept_fits: The wage concepts capture base rates, wage progression, and scheduled increases, but these MOAs define incentive/commission compensation structures and plan administration rather than base wage or wage increase terms.
CBA_04.evidence: Public Communications Incentive Compensation Plan describes base wage plus commission and objective-based quarterly commissions; Retail Sales Consultant plan appears as a separate compensation plan MOA.
CBA_04.scoreability_note: Requires separate treatment from base wage and premium pay; central review should decide whether to normalize incentive compensation.

CBA_04.why_no_frozen_concept_fits: The frozen library has health, retirement, leave, wage, premium, scheduling, safety, union, mobility, and dependent-care concepts, but no employee product/service discount or concession concept.
CBA_04.evidence: Regular employees with six or more months of accredited service are eligible for 50% concession rates for telephone service subject to tariffs and limitations.
CBA_04.scoreability_note: Worker-facing fringe benefit but outside current concept library and scoring modules.

CBA_05.candidate_label: none identified in direct Wave 2 pass
CBA_05.why_not_mapped: No recurring worker-facing object was identified that clearly required a new frozen concept ID for this narrow Wave 2 stress test.

CBA_08.candidate_label: Fringe-benefit delinquency enforcement and job-stoppage remedy
CBA_08.recommended_action: Add a concept under enforcement or wage/fringe security if recurring in construction CBAs.
CBA_08.why_not_in_core_library: Current library has grievance/arbitration and union rights, but not a clean concept for benefit-fund collection enforcement.

CBA_08.recommended_action: Queue for recurrence check before adding a concept.
CBA_08.why_not_in_core_library: Current library does not include small equipment-loss reimbursement protections.

CBA_08.candidate_label: Retiree return-to-work with continued pension benefits where permitted
CBA_08.recommended_action: Treat as novelty until recurrence is known.
CBA_08.why_not_in_core_library: Current retirement concepts do not include post-retirement work rights.

CBA_09.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: This attachment looks like a subgroup-coverage or carve-out note rather than one of the frozen concept objects.
The side letter is attachment-specific governance context and does not map cleanly to a frozen scored entitlement.
It relates to subcontracting/work-preservation, but the side-letter format suggests a separate branch rather than the main article object.
CBA_09.notes: Do not collapse into the main contracting-out record automatically.

source_pointer.quote_or_pointer: Hours of Work - Overtime Compensation for Alternative Schedules
source_pointer.heading: HOURS OF WORK - OVERTIME COMPENSATION FOR ALTERNATIVE SCHEDULES

CBA_09.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: The page shard is heading-only, so the entitlement text is not stable enough to map to the overtime module.
It is a subgroup-status attachment and not a stand-alone frozen concept in the library.
CBA_09.notes: Keep as context for seniority and discipline, not a separate score.

CBA_09.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: The operative terms appear to live in external plan materials, so this is a boundary marker rather than a scored benefit object.
The side letter is a wage-schedule attachment and should not be forced into the raw wage table or increase module.

CBA_10.reason_no_frozen_concept: The frozen concept library does not contain a general allowance/reimbursement concept. Mileage, lodging, meal, and overtime-meal reimbursements are worker-facing economic terms but should not be mapped to wages or premium pay.
CBA_10.suggested_review: Consider a future allowance/reimbursement concept or keep as expense context.

CBA_10.reason_no_frozen_concept: The frozen concept library does not contain a transportation fringe/pre-tax payroll deduction concept. It is not a wage increase, premium, dependent care, or health benefit.
CBA_10.suggested_review: Consider whether commuter benefits should be a future optional/context benefit concept.

CBA_12.candidate_label: PRN Nurse subgroup classification with carve-outs
CBA_12.notes: Keep separate from the main bargaining-unit record.

source_pointer.heading: ARTICLE 41, PRN NURSE
source_pointer.quote_or_pointer: ARTICLE 41, PRN NURSE ARTICLE 41, PRN NURSE page 75

CBA_12.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: The article creates a separate subgroup with its own wage and scheduling rules and excludes many other articles.
The frozen library does not include a successors concept, and the language is about placement after divestiture rather than ordinary recall.

CBA_14.note: No novel measurement object was identified that lacked a frozen concept-library fit.

CBA_15.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: The frozen library has no apparel or laundering-benefit concept.

CBA_15.notes: Named insured coverage while acting within scope of duties.
CBA_15.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: The library has health and retirement concepts, but not a liability-insurance concept.
The library has training and scope concepts, but no moonlighting or outside-work approval object.

The library has training, but not a license-reimbursement concept.
The library has schedule structure, but not a standalone work-year/duty-day table concept.

CBA_17.why_not_mapped: The CBA guarantees academic freedom and limits censorship/restraints on inquiry, but the frozen concept library has no academic-freedom or professional-autonomy concept. It is not mapped to discipline or grievance because the provision is a separate professional-rights object.
CBA_17.suggested_review: Consider whether academic freedom/professional autonomy should become a sector-specific optional context concept for education CBAs.

CBA_17.candidate_object: student discipline authority and student removal conference
CBA_17.why_not_mapped: The article gives teachers primary disciplinary responsibility, right to recommend suspension, and ability to send disruptive students to the principal with a follow-up conference. It is related to safety/working conditions but does not fit the frozen assault, PPE/unsafe-work, or discipline-of-worker concepts cleanly.
CBA_17.suggested_review: Consider an education-sector classroom authority/student discipline working-condition concept if recurring.

CBA_19.notes: Relevant for labor-relations structure, not direct generosity measurement.

source_pointer.heading: ARTICLE III NO STRIKE--NO LOCKOUT
source_pointer.quote_or_pointer: There shall be no strike, stoppage of work, or lockout during the term of the Agreement.

CBA_19.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: The library has grievance and discipline concepts, but not a standalone labor-peace or work-stoppage prohibition concept.

CBA_19.notes: Could affect wages or benefits indirectly, but the operative object is contract comparison rather than a direct entitlement.

source_pointer.heading: ARTICLE XVII FAVORED NATIONS CLAUSE
source_pointer.quote_or_pointer: Favored nations clause allows adoption of more advantageous terms from comparator employers in the same business.

CBA_19.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: The concept library does not contain a comparability-trigger or favored-nations governance object.

source_pointer.heading: ARTICLE XVIII SAVINGS CLAUSE
source_pointer.quote_or_pointer: If part of the Agreement is invalidated, the remainder stays in full force and effect.

CBA_19.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: This is a severability rule, not a worker-facing entitlement or protection in the current library.

CBA_19.notes: Do not treat this as a substantive retirement-plan generosity object.

source_pointer.heading: ARTICLE XX INDIVIDUAL RETIREMENT ACCOUNT (IRA)
source_pointer.quote_or_pointer: Employer shall deduct employee-designated amounts and forward them to a single individual retirement account at no cost other than payroll deduction.

CBA_19.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: The retirement concept library covers pension/annuity benefit design, but this clause is only a payroll-deduction mechanism for a single IRA.

source_pointer.quote_or_pointer: Association agrees not to call, instigate, engage in, encourage, or sanction strikes or related work stoppages; Employer agrees not to lock out covered employees.
source_pointer.heading: ARTICLE XXVII - NO STRIKE, NO LOCKOUT

CBA_20.candidate_label: No-strike / no-lockout and narrowed strike-discipline review
CBA_20.why_existing_concepts_do_not_fit: The frozen library has grievance, discipline, and union-access concepts, but no standalone labor-peace/no-strike/no-lockout concept. The narrowed review exception is carried inside the just-cause record, but the labor-peace obligation itself remains outside the library.
CBA_20.notes: Do not score as union access or grievance generosity; it mostly restricts concerted activity and employer lockout.
